Tool_goal_sip_Description: How much SIP per month to reach your target. Step-up SIP and inflation-adjusted goals.
Tool_goal_sip_Subtitle: Required monthly SIP for a target corpus
Tool_goal_sip_LearnText: Enter your goal amount, time horizon, and expected return. The calculator works backwards to the monthly SIP you need using the standard annuity formula.
Tool_goal_sip_FormulaNote: Uses sipRequired from compound interest math. Higher expected returns lower the required SIP — but returns are not guaranteed.
Tool_goal_sip_RateHint: Equity SIPs often use 10–14% illustrative CAGR; debt lower.
Tool_goal_sip_Result_TargetFootnote: Corpus you want at the end
Tool_goal_sip_ScenarioSubtitle: Same goal — how SIP changes with return
Tool_goal_sip_ExampleStep_2: Assume 12% p.a. expected return → monthly rate r = 1%.
Tool_goal_sip_ExampleStep_4: You get the monthly amount to invest every month.
Tool_goal_sip_ExampleStep_5: Compare lower/higher return scenarios — lower return needs a larger SIP.
Tool_goal_sip_Step_1: Convert annual return to monthly rate r.
Tool_goal_sip_Step_4: Total invested = SIP × n (before market gains).
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Abbreviation: Goal SIP — SIP needed for a target
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Purpose: Find the monthly SIP that can grow to your goal amount.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_WhyKnow: Guessing a SIP amount often undershoots the goal. Working backwards from the target is clearer.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_RealLife: House down payment, wedding corpus, child education fund.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Goal_2: See total amount you will invest
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When you know the SIP and want future value.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Alt_Sip_Pros: Shows wealth creation from a fixed SIP.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Alt_Sip_Cons: Does not answer “how much SIP for my goal?”
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Alt_Goal_When: When inflation-adjusted goals and secure/volatile bands matter.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_When: When you invest once instead of monthly.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra focuses on reverse SIP math with clear return scenarios for the same goal.
Tool_goal_sip_Info_Motto: Start from the goal — then size the SIP.
Tool_stp_Description: Systematic Transfer Plan from debt to equity. STP vs SIP vs lumpsum comparison.
Tool_stp_Subtitle: Move monthly from debt to equity
Tool_stp_LearnText: Park a lumpsum in a debt fund, then transfer a fixed amount every month into equity. You stay invested in both while rupee-cost averaging into equity.
Tool_stp_FormulaNote: Each month debt and equity earn their assumed returns, then the STP amount moves from debt to equity (capped by debt balance).
Tool_stp_ScenarioSubtitle: Same STP schedule, different equity returns
Tool_stp_ExampleStep_1: Start with ₹5,00,000 in a debt fund.
Tool_stp_ExampleStep_2: Transfer ₹10,000 every month to equity for 24 months.
Tool_stp_ExampleStep_3: Debt balance earns ~6%; equity instalments grow at ~12%.
Tool_stp_ExampleStep_4: After 24 months, see equity corpus + leftover debt.
Tool_stp_ExampleStep_5: Compare with investing the full lumpsum in equity at once (higher risk).
Tool_stp_Step_1: Grow debt and equity by monthly returns.
Tool_stp_Step_2: Move min(STP, debt balance) into equity.
Tool_stp_Step_4: Report equity value, debt residual, and total.
Tool_stp_Info_Purpose: Project debt→equity monthly transfers and ending balances.
Tool_stp_Info_WhyKnow: STPs reduce timing risk vs investing a lumpsum in equity on day one.
Tool_stp_Info_RealLife: Bonus or FD maturity moved gradually into equity funds.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Sip_When: When money comes from salary, not a parked lumpsum.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Sip_Pros: No need for a large starting corpus.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Swp_When: When you withdraw from a corpus instead of transferring.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_When: When you accept full market timing risk.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_Example: Invest entire ₹5 lakh in equity on day one.
Tool_stp_Info_Alt_Lumpsum_Pros: Maximum time in market if markets rise.
Tool_stp_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra simulates month-by-month debt and equity balances with a clear residual debt view.
Tool_stp_Info_Motto: Transfer steadily — stay invested in both.
Tool_gold_investment_Description: Compare SGB vs ETF vs physical gold. Returns, tax, and portfolio allocation.
Tool_gold_investment_Subtitle: SIP or lumpsum gold — digital vs physical
Tool_gold_investment_LearnText: Project gold’s future value with SIP or lumpsum at an assumed appreciation rate. Physical jewellery often loses making charges upfront; digital gold / ETFs invest closer to the full amount.
Tool_gold_investment_FormulaNote: Digital uses full SIP/lumpsum FV. Physical reduces the invested amount by making charges %, then compounds at the same gold appreciation.
Tool_gold_investment_AppreciationHint: Long-term gold returns vary; 6–10% is a common planning band.
Tool_gold_investment_MakingHint: Typical jewellery making charges ~3–15% depending on design.
Tool_gold_investment_CompareNote: At {0} making charges, you pay about {1} extra on physical — digital ends ahead by roughly {2} in this projection.
Tool_gold_investment_BreakdownSubtitle: Assuming full amount invested (no making charges)
Tool_gold_investment_ExampleStep_2: Digital gold compounds the full SIP at 8% p.a.
Tool_gold_investment_ExampleStep_3: Physical jewellery: ~8% making means only ~₹4,600 buys gold each month.
Tool_gold_investment_ExampleStep_4: Compare ending digital vs physical values.
Tool_gold_investment_ExampleStep_5: For jewellery, also weigh emotional/use value — not only returns.
Tool_gold_investment_Step_2: Grow digital investment at gold appreciation.
Tool_gold_investment_Step_3: For physical, reduce principal by making % first.
Tool_gold_investment_Step_4: Compare digital vs physical ending values.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Purpose: Estimate gold’s future value and the cost of making charges on physical gold.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_WhyKnow: Making charges and spreads can erase years of gold appreciation on jewellery.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_RealLife: Wedding jewellery vs digital gold / gold ETF for portfolio allocation.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Goal_1: Project gold SIP or lumpsum value
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Goal_2: Compare digital vs physical after making charges
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Alt_Fd_When: For capital protection and known interest.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Alt_Elss_When: When tax-saving equity fits better than gold.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra highlights making-charge drag on physical gold versus digital/ETF investment.
Tool_gold_investment_Info_Motto: Gold can hedge — making charges still cost.
